_doc: Adversarial per-paper re-verification of every extracted finding against its raw source text. Assumed the first pass was wrong until proven. Quote fabrication additionally gated by tools/verify_quotes.py.

corrections.recorded: Twelve of the thirteen had left-sided seizures; N=84
corrections.correct: dominant (left-sided); 12 of 13 left-sided seizures, left language dominance in 10 of 13
corrections.note: DIRECTION ERROR: recorded direction 'nondominant' contradicts the cited datum. Koerner & Laxer found ictal speech in 12 of 13 patients with LEFT-sided (dominant) seizures, with left-sided language dominance in 10/13. For THIS study the direction is dominant/left, not nondominant. (The paper's Table-1 '83% nondominant' for ictal speech derives from a DIFFERENT study, Gabr [145].)

corrections.paper: Serafetinides EA, Falconer MA. Speech disturbances in temporal lobe seizures: a study in 100 epileptic patients submitted to anterior temporal lobectomy. Brain 1963;86(2):333-346.
corrections.phenomenon: Ictal speech automatism reproduced by right amygdala stimulation (addendum case)
corrections.recorded: single case: recurrent English utterance 'I beg your pardon', consistent right sphenoidal EEG focus (recessive lobe) in right-hander
corrections.correct: Addendum case reproduced the automatism by a METRAZOL (chemical) activation, NOT amygdala stimulation. Single case of ictal speech automatism in an acquired language (English): a 38-year-old Pole; utterance 'I beg your pardon' at seizure onset; EEG focus consistently at the RIGHT sphenoidal area (recessive lobe). Handedness NOT stated in the addendum.
corrections.note: MISATTRIBUTION: this paper's addendum has no amygdala stimulation anywhere in the text (grep-verified); reproduction was by metrazol activation. Electrical reproduction by RIGHT AMYGDALA stimulation belongs to a DIFFERENT study (Driver, Falconer & Serafetinides, Neurology 1964). Also 'right-hander' is unsupported - patient is described only as 'a Pole aged 38'. Direction (recessive/nondominant, right sphenoidal) remains correct.

corrections.paper: Bonini F, McGonigal A, Trebuchon A, Gavaret M, Bartolomei F, Giusiano B, Chauvel P. Frontal lobe seizures: from clinical semiology to localization. Epilepsia 2014;55(2):264-277 (doi:10.1111/epi.12490)
corrections.correct: v-test 2.09 is correct numerically, but in Group 4 this is most likely a NEGATIVE (typically ABSENT) sign, not a present/defining one
corrections.note: VALUE 2.09 matches Table 1 exactly. BUT the present/absent classification is questionable: in Groups 1-3 the table lists positive signs in DESCENDING order then negative (absent) signs in ASCENDING order; in Group 4 the descending positive arm ends at 2.47 (Hyperkinetic), then 2.09 (Impairment) then 3.39 (Elementary motor signs*) form the ascending NEGATIVE arm. Elementary motor signs is confirmed absent in Group 4 by body text ('Elementary motor signs never occurred'), so 2.09 sits on the negative/absent arm. Clinically, ventromedial/orbitofrontal fear seizures typically PRESERVE consciousness, consistent with impairment being ABSENT. Extraction labeled it 'characteristic present' - likely a misclassification of an absent sign. Low-to-moderate confidence given the garbled table; flag for review.

corrections.paper: Bonini F, McGonigal A, Trebuchon A, Gavaret M, Bartolomei F, Giusiano B, Chauvel P. Frontal lobe seizures: from clinical semiology to localization. Epilepsia 2014;55(2):264-277 (doi:10.1111/epi.12490)
corrections.correct: value 2.09 correct; likely a NEGATIVE/absent sign in Group 4, not present
corrections.note: Same issue as in the _2014 file: value matches Table 1 but present/absent classification is questionable (sits on the ascending negative arm 2.09->3.39; Elementary motor signs confirmed absent by body text).
